prompt:
The roaster-queue controller for our coffee shop won't compile since a teammate started 'asyncifying' it before going on leave, and I'm not fluent enough in Task-land to untangle what they half-did. `dotnet test` says:

  Roaster.cs(38,16): error CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'int' to 'System.Threading.Tasks.Task<int>'
  Roaster.cs(58,13): error CS4008: Cannot await 'void'
  Roaster.cs(66,17): error CS1503: Argument 1: cannot convert from 'System.Threading.Tasks.Task<RoastQueue.RoastProfile>' to 'RoastQueue.RoastProfile'
  Roaster.cs(86,9): error CS1997: Since 'Roaster.ShutDownAsync()' is an async method that returns 'Task', a return keyword must not be followed by an object expression
  RoasterTests.cs(25,20): error CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'void' to 'System.Threading.Tasks.Task'
  RoasterTests.cs(39,67): error CS0029: Cannot implicitly convert type 'void' to 'System.Threading.Tasks.Task'
  RoasterTests.cs(39,67): error CS1662: Cannot convert lambda expression to intended delegate type because some of the return types in the block are not implicitly convertible to the delegate return type

Note the last three point INTO the test file, but the tests are our spec and are not to be edited — whatever the tests expect the signatures to be, that's what the roaster should expose.

One more thing, because it bit us on the shop floor already: even back when this built, the 'next bag label' feature would freeze the panel whenever the profile service was slow, and whatever was causing that seems to still be in there. The tests cover that scenario, so please make sure the whole suite is green, not just compiling. Injected preheater/profile hooks make everything drivable from the tests — no sleeps, no real hardware. Don't touch RoasterTests.cs, the csproj, or .gitignore.
notes: 5C3 item 5: async triage, .NET 10 / SDK 10.0.302. TOOLCHAIN PIVOT, probed 2026-07-15: this SDK's Roslyn no longer emits CS1998 (async-without-await) AT ALL — verified with a minimal repro (bare `async Task<int> D() { return 1; }` builds with 0 warnings even under -warnaserror). The catalog's 'async method without await (warning-as-error)' shape is therefore realized as the two modern wrap-confusion ERRORS instead: DepthAsync declared Task<int> returning bare int (CS0029, fix Task.FromResult) and ShutDownAsync declared `async` while returning Task.CompletedTask (CS1997, fix: drop async). Remaining planted errors: async void RunNextAsync awaited in RunAllAsync (CS4008) and asserted as Task by the frozen tests (CS0029/CS1662 pointing INTO RoasterTests.cs — deliberate: signatures are pinned by the spec-tests, prompt says so); AnnounceAsync passing un-awaited Task<RoastProfile> into Publish(RoastProfile) (CS1503, fix: await). Post-compile teeth: NextLabelAsync blocks on _profiles(order).Result — the TCS-driven test captures the raw task via a Task.Run(Action) wrapper (avoiding Task.Run's Func<Task> unwrap) and Task.WhenAny against a 5s Delay: the fixed impl returns a pending task in microseconds (no timing sensitivity on the pass path), the .Result impl deterministically loses the WhenAny and the TCS is completed afterwards so the blocked thread never leaks; failure message names the freeze symptom. All phase-transition tests are TCS-driven with continuations observed only after awaiting the method's task — no sleeps anywhere. Chain-reveal: none; all 7 errors report in round 1 (confirmed).
